body:
(So somehow my FNV trans shitpost lead to this 🤷‍♀️)

The bottle was sweating almost as much as I was when the bartender slid it across the warped countertop—condensation tracing lazy paths through the dust. Two straws reached out of the glass bottle, bent slightly above the neck one pointing to each side of the bottle. “I hear it’s the last one in the fridge,” my friend said, like that explained why we had to share it, why her knee kept brushing mine under the bullet-scarred diner table. Outside, there was a beautiful Mojave sunset visible if I had looked out the window, but all I could focus on is her lips and just how close they would be to mine if we were to sip at the same time.

We both leaned in, each of leaning in towards our respective straws. I was entranced with both the eyes looking back at me and the thoughts that came from watching the motion of her lips as she put the straw in her mouth. The taste of Sunset Sarsaparilla was sweet and wonderfully familiar, but I had other tastes lingering in my thoughts.

She pulled back for a moment, biting her lip after letting out a soft chuckle. “You’re staring,” she teased, whispering in my ear. There was something about the way she said it that made my stomach flip…like she wanted me to keep looking. I turned bright pink at her noticed and leaned in for another sip, hoping to buy time for a response. The sarsaparilla fizzed against my tongue, bubbles almost feeling like sparks…or maybe it was just my nerves.

Her knee pressed firmer against mine beneath the table. The touches were deliberate, questioning. A smirk grew in the corners of her lips, and I wondered how much longer I could pretend this was just about a drink, especially with the delicious thoughts running through my head. The Sunset Sarsaparilla was almost gone, but neither of us seemed in a hurry to finish it…it was obvious we had other things on our minds….
